Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor (anthocyanin regulatory factor; inferred from common use of maize Lc in anthocyanin engineering, but not explicitly stated in excerpt)
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-expression with other transgenes in rice endosperm enables/boosts anthocyanin biosynthesis by activating/upregulating endogenous anthocyanin-pathway genes, leading to anthocyanin accumulation and increased antioxidant activity in grain.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Increases antioxidant capacity of grain extracts (DPPH/TEAC).
Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm of PER line (semi-quantitative RT–PCR).
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-gene transgenic expression (PER construct including ZmLc); semi-quantitative RT–PCR of transgenes and endogenous pathway genes; antioxidant activity assay (DPPH/TEAC).
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Metabolic engineering/biofortification of rice endosperm to produce anthocyanin-enriched grains with enhanced antioxidant activity.
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmLc was assembled into a multi-gene construct (pYLTAC380GW-2G and later into larger stacks) for engineering anthocyanin production. 2. In PER rice endosperm, transgenes (including ZmLc) were highly expressed and endogenous anthocyanin-related genes were activated/upregulated. 3. Anthocyanin extracts from engineered grains showed increased total antioxidant activity (TEAC/DPPH assay) versus wild type.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: ZmLc was incorporated via iterative multi-gene assembly using loxP/Cre-based in vivo recombination and homing endonuclease selection (I-SceI/PI-SceI) into transformation-competent vectors (e.g., pYLTAC380GW series).

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: When expressed as a transgene in rice endosperm as part of a multigene stack, contributes to engineering/activating the anthocyanin biosynthetic program, enabling anthocyanin accumulation and increased antioxidant activity in grain.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Increases antioxidant activity (TEAC/DPPH scavenging activity) in grain extracts.
Genes.Expression_Pattern: High expression of the ZmPl transgene in endosperm of developing seeds in PER line (semi-quantitative RT-PCR).
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic multi-gene assembly and expression (stacked construct); semi-quantitative RT-PCR of transgenes and endogenous pathway genes; antioxidant activity assay (TEAC/DPPH).
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Metabolic engineering/biofortification of rice endosperm to produce anthocyanin-enriched (purple) grain with elevated antioxidant capacity.
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPl was assembled into a transformation-competent acceptor vector as the first gene cassette (pYLTAC380GW-1G) for iterative multigene stacking. 2. In PER rice, the transgenes (including ZmPl) were expressed at high levels in developing endosperm and coincided with activation/upregulation of multiple endogenous anthocyanin-biosynthetic genes. 3. PER grains showed increased antioxidant activity, consistent with engineered anthocyanin accumulation in endosperm.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: ZmPl was incorporated via iterative in vivo recombination (loxP-based) in E. coli NS3529 using donor vector pYL322d1-ZmPl; positive recombinants were identified by PCR (380F + gene-specific reverse primer) and NotI digestion; gene function not dissected singly (reported in the context of a multigene stack).

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required as part of the introduced anthocyanin biosynthetic gene set for anthocyanin accumulation (C3G and P3G) in rice endosperm; lines lacking SsCHS (together with SsCHI and SsF3′H) showed lighter purple grains.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing seeds; polished grain endosperm
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Increased antioxidant activity in grains (TEAC/DPPH scavenging activity).
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Transgene expression detected in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination) in PER line by semi-quantitative RT–PCR.
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-gene transgenic pathway reconstitution in rice endosperm (TGSII system); transgene presence/absence associated with grain color phenotype; semi-quantitative RT–PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling of anthocyanins; antioxidant activity assay (TEAC/DPPH).
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Metabolic engineering target enabling production of anthocyanin-enriched (antioxidant) rice endosperm/polished grains.
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsCHS (from Coleus; GenBank EF522149) was included among structural genes assembled for anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. PER/ZJM transgenic rice accumulated anthocyanins (C3G and P3G) in endosperm; a line missing SsCHS (with SsCHI and SsF3′H) showed lighter purple grains. 3. Anthocyanin-enriched grains showed increased antioxidant activity compared with ZH11.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: SsCHS was delivered together with SsF3′H in the same assembly module (pYL322d2-SsF3′H-SsCHS) during iterative multi-gene assembly to generate the 8-gene construct (pYLTAC380GW-8G).

Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chalcone isomerase (CHI) enzyme (flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis structural gene)
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for engineered anthocyanin accumulation (cyanidin 3-O-glucoside and peonidin 3-O-glucoside) in rice endosperm; loss of SsCHI (with SsCHS and SsF3'H) is associated with lighter purple grains in a transgenic line.
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in developing endosperm of transgenic rice (PER) at 7 and 14 days after pollination (as a transgene in the engineered pathway).
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-gene transgenic reconstitution of anthocyanin pathway in rice endosperm; transgene presence/absence associated with grain color phenotype; HPLC anthocyanin profiling; antioxidant (TEAC) assay; semi-quantitative RT-PCR for transgene expression.
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Metabolic engineering/biofortification of rice endosperm to produce anthocyanin-enriched (purple) grains with increased antioxidant activity.
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsCHI was included as a Coleus structural gene in an eight-gene assembly to engineer anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. Transgenic rice grains accumulated anthocyanins (C3G and P3G) in endosperm; a line lacking SsCHI (along with SsCHS and SsF3'H) showed lighter purple grain coloration. 3. SsCHI transcripts were detected in developing endosperm of a homozygous PER line, supporting its role in the engineered pathway that increased antioxidant activity.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accession list notes 'ScCHI' in the text, but the gene referred to in the engineering/figure is SsCHI; SsCHI and SsDFR were assembled together in donor vector pYL322d2-SsCHI-SsDFR during multi-round assembly.

Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-gene pathway assembly into pYLTAC380GW (TGSII vector system); transgene stacking/construct generation
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsF3H was included as one of the structural genes for anthocyanin biosynthesis from Coleus and deposited to GenBank (KY402224). 2. SsF3H was assembled into the multi-gene TGSII donor/acceptor vector system (pYL322d1-SsF3H) as part of an 8-gene anthocyanin pathway construct (pYLTAC380GW-8G).
Genes.Other_Important_Info: SsF3H was assembled in the third and fifth rounds of iterative multi-gene assembly using the TGSII system; other pathway genes mentioned include SsCHS, ScCHI, SsF3′H, SsDFR, and SsANS.

Genes.Variety: Zhonghua 11 (ZH11) (rice background used for endosperm expression analysis)
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm (developing seeds) in engineered rice PER line
Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression detected in endosperm of developing seeds (PER line PER-Z#3) by semi-quantitative RT–PCR (transgenes collectively; SsF3'H not individually resolved).
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic multigene pathway assembly and expression in rice; semi-quantitative RT–PCR of developing endosperm to confirm transgene expression and activation/upregulation of endogenous anthocyanin-related genes
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Engineering anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm (nutritional/quality enhancement) using multigene transgene stacking approach
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsF3'H (Coleus structural anthocyanin gene) sequence was submitted to GenBank (KY402225). 2. SsF3'H was assembled with SsCHS into the multigene donor vector (pYL322d2-SsF3'H-SsCHS) and ultimately into an 8-gene anthocyanin engineering construct for rice. 3. In engineered rice PER endosperm, transgenes were highly expressed and endogenous anthocyanin-pathway genes were activated/upregulated (SsF3'H included as part of the transgene set).
Genes.Other_Important_Info: SsF3'H is referred to as one of the Coleus structural genes for anthocyanin biosynthesis; specific enzymatic role, localization, promoter used, and SsF3'H-specific metabolite effects were not detailed in the provided excerpts.

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes (as one of the assembled anthocyanin-pathway structural transgenes) to successful reconstitution of anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm, enabling anthocyanin accumulation in transgenic endosperm rice lines.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice seeds (polished grains/endosperm cells)
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-gene transgenic pathway reconstruction in rice endosperm; HPLC-based anthocyanin profiling (C3G, P3G) in grains; grain color/section phenotyping; antioxidant (TEAC) assay
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Metabolic engineering target for producing anthocyanin-enriched rice endosperm (purple endosperm rice) with elevated antioxidant activity (nutritional/quality enhancement).
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsDFR was assembled into a multi-gene construct (pYL322d2-SsCHI-SsDFR; incorporated into pYLTAC380GW-8G) to reconstitute anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. Transgenic rice lines carrying the engineered pathway accumulated anthocyanins (C3G and P3G) specifically in endosperm cells and showed increased antioxidant capacity in grains.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: SsDFR was introduced as part of an 8-gene anthocyanin pathway engineering strategy because key endogenous rice genes (including OsDFR) were reported as defective and/or pathway genes were silent/low in endosperm; PER-Z#2 lacking SsCHS/SsCHI/SsF3'H showed lighter purple grains, indicating pathway gene dosage/composition affects pigmentation intensity (SsDFR not specifically singled out in that line).

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Complementation of missing/low endosperm expression of native OsANS as part of a multi-gene stack, enabling completion of the engineered anthocyanin biosynthesis pathway and accumulation of anthocyanins in rice endosperm (purple endosperm).
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice seeds (grains)
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Increased hydrophilic antioxidant activity in grains (health-promoting dietary antioxidants).
Genes.Expression_Pattern: High expression of transgenes in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination) in PER lines.
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-gene transgenic stacking (TGSII) including SsANS; transgene expression verified by semi-quantitative RT-PCR; metabolite profiling by HPLC for C3G and P3G; grain phenotype (purple endosperm) and antioxidant (TEAC) assays.
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Biofortified rice germplasm with enhanced anthocyanins/antioxidant activity in edible endosperm (potential health-promoting food).
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsANS (GenBank EF522159) from Coleus was included to replace/compensate for endogenous OsANS that is not expressed in rice endosperm. 2. Expression of stacked transgenes including SsANS in developing endosperm enabled production of anthocyanins (C3G and P3G) in polished rice grains (purple endosperm). 3. PER grains showed increased antioxidant activity associated with anthocyanin accumulation.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous OsANS is functional but not activated in endosperm by the introduced regulatory genes; therefore a Coleus homolog (SsANS) was required to complete the pathway in the engineered endosperm.

Genes.Expression_Pattern: Used as a highly expressed reference gene for semi-quantitative RT–PCR in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination) and developing seeds/endosperm assays.
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsActin1 (Os03g0718100) was used as the internal reference gene for semi-quantitative RT–PCR in developing rice endosperm. 2. OsActin1 is described as highly expressed relative to many transgenes/endogenous anthocyanin-pathway genes measured in the study.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primers for OsActin1 and other genes are listed in Supplemental Table 2 (per excerpt).

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated in purple endosperm rice (PER) lines and functions as an endogenous component of the MBW regulatory complex that enhances/activates expression of endogenous anthocyanin biosynthesis-related genes, supporting anthocyanin accumulation in endosperm.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity in rice grains (increased TEAC in PER grains)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Upregulated in developing endosperm of PER line (PER-Z#3) compared with ZH11; described as probably induced by the MBW complex
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic pathway engineering (PER/ZJM lines) combined with semi-quantitative RT-PCR of endogenous genes; anthocyanin detection/quantification by HPLC
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Supports engineering/biofortification of rice endosperm with anthocyanins to generate purple endosperm rice with elevated antioxidant activity.
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWD40 (endogenous WD40) is upregulated in PER developing endosperm, likely via the engineered MBW complex. 2. OsWD40 acts as an endogenous regulatory component together with transgenic ZmLc and ZmPl to rebuild the MBW complex in rice endosperm. 3. Rebuilt MBW complex enhances/activates endogenous anthocyanin pathway-related genes, contributing to anthocyanin accumulation in endosperm grains.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsC1 (endogenous MYB) was not activated in PER; authors propose using transgenic ZmLc and ZmPl together with endogenous OsWD40 to reconstitute MBW regulation.

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsC1 is silent/lowly expressed in rice endosperm and was not activated in the engineered Purple Endosperm Rice (PER); anthocyanin accumulation in PER occurred without activation of OsC1.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm (developing seed endosperm cells; polished grain endosperm fraction)
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydrophilic antioxidant activity in grains (increased TEAC); health-promoting antioxidant in human diet
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Silent or low expression in endosperm of white-grained rice varieties (ZH11); not activated in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination) of PER-Z#3
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semi-quantitative RT-PCR showing OsC1 not activated in PER endosperm; anthocyanin profiling by HPLC in PER grains
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsC1 (endogenous MYB) is silent/lowly expressed in the endosperm of the white-grained cultivar ZH11. 2. In engineered PER endosperm, multiple endogenous anthocyanin-pathway genes were activated/upregulated, but OsC1 was not activated. 3. Anthocyanin accumulation (C3G and P3G) in PER endosperm occurred without OsC1 activation, indicating OsC1 is not required/engaged in this engineered endosperm anthocyanin program.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous OsWD40 was upregulated in PER, probably by the rebuilt MBW complex formed by transgenic ZmLc and ZmPl with OsWD40; OsC1 did not respond similarly.

Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chalcone synthase (CHS); type III polyketide synthase (chalcone/stilbene synthase family)
Genes.Variety: Zhonghua 11 (ZH11); black-grained rice cultivar BGR1 (comparative control)
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Engineered expression of regulatory transgenes activated endogenous OsCHS in developing endosperm, contributing to de novo anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm (purple endosperm) and increased antioxidant capacity of grains.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature grains (polished grains) in PER lines
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity in grain extracts (higher TEAC/DPPH scavenging activity)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Activated/upregulated in developing endosperm of transgenic purple endosperm rice (PER) compared with low/silent expression in ZH11 endosperm
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic pathway engineering with regulatory genes; semi-quantitative RT–PCR showing activation of endogenous OsCHS; metabolite profiling by HPLC identifying C3G/P3G; antioxidant activity assay (TEAC/DPPH)
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Creation of marker-free purple endosperm rice germplasm with elevated anthocyanins and antioxidant activity (nutritional/functional quality improvement)
Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Grain weight reduced by ~25% compared with ZH11; most other growth characteristics similar to parental varieties
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endogenous OsCHS (normally silent/low in ZH11 endosperm) was activated in PER developing endosperm by introduced regulatory genes. 2. Activation of OsCHS occurred as part of reconstituting the anthocyanin pathway, resulting in endosperm accumulation of anthocyanins (C3G and P3G). 3. PER grains showed increased antioxidant capacity consistent with anthocyanin accumulation.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transgenic structural genes could further enhance anthocyanin biosynthesis even though endogenous OsCHS/OsCHI/OsF3'OH were activated by regulatory genes; PER lines accumulated low levels of some flavonoids and proanthocyanidins in endosperm in addition to anthocyanins.

Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chalcone isomerase (CHI) (enzyme of flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis pathway)
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated/upregulated endogenous OsCHI expression in developing endosperm in engineered purple endosperm rice lines, contributing to anthocyanin accumulation in endosperm.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice seeds (polished grains/endosperm cells) in PER lines
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Increased antioxidant activity in grains (higher TEAC/DPPH scavenging activity)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Silent or low in endosperm of white-grained rice (ZH11); activated/upregulated in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination) of PER line (PER-Z#3) driven by introduced regulatory network
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endosperm semi-quantitative RT–PCR showing activation/upregulation of endogenous OsCHI in PER; metabolite profiling by HPLC for anthocyanins (C3G, P3G) and quantitative anthocyanin measurement; grain color phenotype (purple endosperm)
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Supports metabolic engineering/biofortification of rice endosperm with anthocyanins to enhance nutritional/antioxidant properties in edible polished grains
Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced grain weight (~25%) in PER lines compared with ZH11; other growth characteristics largely similar to parental varieties
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endogenous OsCHI is normally silent/lowly expressed in white-rice endosperm but becomes activated/upregulated in purple endosperm rice (PER) engineered for anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. Upregulation of OsCHI occurs alongside activation of multiple endogenous anthocyanin-pathway genes in developing endosperm (7–14 DAP) and associates with accumulation of C3G and P3G anthocyanins in the endosperm. 3. PER grains show increased antioxidant capacity relative to ZH11, consistent with enhanced anthocyanin production.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endosperm anthocyanin engineering used regulatory genes (ZmLc, ZmPl) plus endogenous OsWD40 to rebuild an MBW complex; OsC1 was not activated; some functional endogenous structural genes (OsF3H, OsANS, OsF3'5'H, OsLAR) were not activated.

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endogenous OsF3H is functional but not activated in developing endosperm of either ZH11 or engineered Purple Endosperm Rice (PER), indicating it does not contribute via transcriptional activation to engineered endosperm anthocyanin accumulation in this system.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing/mature grain (polished grains) in PER lines
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Not activated (silent/low) in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination) in both ZH11 and PER-Z#3 under the tested conditions
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semi-quantitative RT–PCR of developing endosperm assessing endogenous gene activation status in PER vs ZH11
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsF3H is described as a functional endogenous structural gene in rice. 2. OsF3H was not activated by the introduced regulatory genes in developing endosperm of both ZH11 and PER-Z#3. 3. Engineered endosperm anthocyanin accumulation in PER occurs without detectable activation/upregulation of endogenous OsF3H.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsF3H is grouped with OsANS, OsF3'5'H (OsF30 50 H), and OsLAR as functional endogenous structural genes that remained unactivated despite engineered MBW-regulator-driven pathway activation.

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In the engineered purple endosperm rice (PER), endogenous OsF3'OH expression in developing endosperm was activated/upregulated, supporting anthocyanin accumulation (cyanidin/peonidin glycosides) in endosperm.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing seeds/grains (polished grain endosperm) in PER lines
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity (increased TEAC/DPPH scavenging activity in grain extracts)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Activated/upregulated in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination) in PER lines compared with ZH11, where related genes were silent/low
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic pathway reconstruction in rice endosperm with semi-quantitative RT-PCR of endogenous gene activation and HPLC-based anthocyanin profiling (C3G, P3G)
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Engineering anthocyanin-enriched rice endosperm (biofortified/functional rice with higher antioxidant activity)
Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Grain weight reduced by ~25% in PER lines compared with ZH11 (other growth characteristics largely similar)
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In PER engineered lines, the endogenous anthocyanin-pathway gene OsF3'OH (silent/low in ZH11 endosperm) was activated/upregulated in developing endosperm. 2. PER grains accumulated anthocyanins (C3G and P3G) in endosperm, consistent with activation of structural pathway genes including OsF3'OH. 3. Anthocyanin accumulation in PER increased grain antioxidant activity (TEAC).
Genes.Other_Important_Info: In a lighter-purple PER-Z#2 line, the transgene SsF3'OH was absent, indicating introduced F3'OH activity can contribute to pigmentation level even when endogenous OsF3'OH is activated by regulatory genes.

Genes.Variety: Zhonghua 11 (ZH11); black-grained rice cultivar BGR1 (comparative control)
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endogenous OsDFR is defective in white-grained rice endosperm; functional deficiency is one of the barriers preventing anthocyanin biosynthesis in endosperm unless complemented by transgenic pathway reconstruction.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm (developing and mature polished grains) in engineered PER lines
Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsDFR reported as defective in endosperm of white-grained rice varieties; otherwise NA
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway engineering via multi-gene transgenic reconstruction (PER lines) with metabolite profiling (HPLC for C3G/P3G) and expression analysis (semi-quantitative RT-PCR of pathway genes)
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Identification of defective OsDFR helps select required target genes for engineering/biofortification of anthocyanins in rice endosperm (purple endosperm rice germplasm).
Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Grain weight reduced by ~25% in PER lines compared with ZH11 (growth traits otherwise similar).
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDFR is defective in white-grained rice endosperm, contributing to lack of endosperm anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. Successful reconstruction of anthocyanin production in rice endosperm required selecting target genes based in part on such endogenous defects, enabling accumulation of C3G and P3G in engineered lines. 3. Engineered anthocyanin accumulation increased antioxidant activity in rice grains, but was associated with reduced grain weight (~25%).
Genes.Other_Important_Info: The engineered pathway used a transgenic DFR (SsDFR) as part of an 8-gene stack; OsDFR specifically is discussed as defective rather than being functionally activated/upregulated.

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endogenous OsANS is not expressed (not activated) in developing endosperm of both wild-type ZH11 and engineered PER lines; therefore OsANS does not contribute to engineered endosperm anthocyanin accumulation and had to be complemented by a heterologous ANS (SsANS) to complete anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing seeds; polished grains (endosperm-derived) in PER lines
Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydrophilic antioxidant activity in grain (increased TEAC/antioxidant capacity)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Not activated/expressed in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination) in both ZH11 and PER-Z#3 despite presence of regulatory genes
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semi-quantitative RT–PCR of developing endosperm showing OsANS not activated; multi-gene transgenic pathway reconstruction requiring heterologous SsANS; metabolite profiling by HPLC for anthocyanins (C3G, P3G)
Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Indicates endogenous OsANS is insufficient for endosperm anthocyanin biofortification; endosperm anthocyanin engineering requires supplying a functional/expressed ANS activity (e.g., transgenic ANS) to achieve antioxidant-enriched rice grain
Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsANS is a functional endogenous structural gene but is not activated in rice endosperm by the engineered regulatory module (MBW complex) in either ZH11 or PER-Z#3. 2. Lack of OsANS expression is one limiting step for anthocyanin biosynthesis in endosperm, necessitating introduction of a homologous ANS transgene (SsANS) to complete the pathway. 3. Completion of the pathway (including heterologous ANS activity) enables accumulation of anthocyanins C3G and P3G in endosperm-derived polished grains with increased antioxidant activity.
Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper distinguishes OsANS from the Coleus ANS transgene (SsANS; GenBank for SsANS: EF522159). Four endogenous structural genes (OsF3H, OsANS, OsF3'5'H, OsLAR) were explicitly reported as not activated in endosperm by the introduced regulatory genes.
OsLAR is not described in the provided excerpts beyond being the queried gene name; no functional/metabolic/experimental details are provided here.
